messages.content: on the carousel that third slide 90 minutes just talking about life after work in a recent meeting somehow doesnt feel like its emphasizing the right thing. its hard to put a finger on what that is exactly, but do you know what I mean
Logged for IMG_4168. Likely target: the carousel. For now this note is review guidance; it does not rewrite or publish anything automatically.

on the carousel that third slide 90 minutes just talking about life after work in a recent meeting somehow doesnt feel like its emphasizing the right thing. its hard to put a finger on what that is exactly, but do you know what I mean
Adjusted the third slide to better emphasize the meaning behind the conversation, not just the length.

suggestions.title: Update Slide 3 Number Caption
suggestions.current: Minutes spent just talking about life after work in a recent retirement meeting
suggestions.replacement: Minutes spent exploring what life could look like after work—with no talk about numbers
suggestions.rationale: Shifts focus from the time spent to the unique depth of the conversation, underscoring the emotional and personal side of planning rather than making it seem like time alone matters.

[
  {
    "kind": "cover",
    "eyebrow": "Retirement isn't just math",
    "headline": "The question almost no one asks before retiring",
    "body": "Your investments and taxes may be planned out. But have you stopped to ask what comes next?",
    "index": 1,
    "total": 7
  },
  {
    "kind": "point",
    "eyebrow": "",
    "headline": "We spend years planning the numbers",
    "body": "People focus on income, investments, and taxes before retirement. That is only part of the plan.",
    "index": 2,
    "total": 7
  },
  {
    "kind": "number",
    "number": "90",
    "numberCaption": "Minutes spent just talking about life after work in a recent retirement meeting",
    "index": 3,
    "total": 7
  },
  {
    "kind": "quote",
    "quote": "The real retirement question is: what are you going to do with your days when the emails stop?",
    "attribution": "David Talley",
    "index": 4,

  },
  {
    "kind": "point",
    "eyebrow": "",
    "headline": "Most people are never asked",
    "body": "Some shrug off the question of life after retirement. Some realize they have not thought about it at all.",

    "total": 7

  {
    "kind": "point",

    "headline": "Purpose beyond work matters",
